vBRIEFInfo.description: Update README.md to describe the vBRIEF-centric flow: scope vBRIEFs are the source of truth; SPECIFICATION.md is a rendered view.

plan.title: docs(readme): README still describes SPECIFICATION.md as primary output instead of vBRIEF

narratives.Problem: README.md described SPECIFICATION.md as the primary output of the setup/spec flow. In the v0.20 vBRIEF-centric model SPECIFICATION.md is a rendered artifact; the source of truth is `vbrief/specification.vbrief.json` plus scope vBRIEFs in lifecycle folders. If a user edits SPECIFICATION.md directly, their changes are overwritten on the next `task spec:render`.
narratives.Action: Rewrite README Step 2 (`run bootstrap` output) to reference PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json, rename Step 3 to 'Generate a Scope vBRIEF' and describe the vBRIEF-centric flow, update Step 4 build example to read vbrief/PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json and the scope vBRIEFs, add a source-of-truth / rendered-view paragraph, fix the Rule Precedence diagram and Rule Hierarchy list, update SDD diagram and description to say vBRIEF instead of SPECIFICATION.md, update example workflow diagrams to read PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json. Also surface `deft/run upgrade` in the command list and add a brownfield note linking to docs/BROWNFIELD.md.
narratives.Outcome: New users reading README understand that vBRIEFs are authoritative and .md files are generated, preventing the 'edit SPECIFICATION.md and lose work' footgun.

items.title: Rename Step 3 to 'Generate a Scope vBRIEF' with vBRIEF-centric explanation
Update Step 4 build example and add brownfield pointer to docs/BROWNFIELD.md
Fix Rule Precedence mermaid + Rule Hierarchy list to reference vbrief/* files
Update SDD diagrams and text to reference specification.vbrief.json
Update example workflow diagrams (Existing Go Project, code review) to read PROJECT-DEFINITION.vbrief.json
